author: kevaundray
content:
Context: EWS was originally created as a layer between proof systems that would:

- Connect to multiple CLs and ELs
- Listen for new blocks from the CL
- Whenever a CL receives a block, EWS would check to see if it has already created a proof
- If not, ask the EL for an execution witness and generate the other EL inputs needed for the proof
- Request proof from "provers"
- Send proof to CL

So from the CLs perspective:

- They receive a block and wait for a proof to arrive.
- After X seconds someone sends them a proof via their RPC endpoint
- They validate the proof and forward it to the rest of the network

author: kevaundray
content: EWS = Execution Witness Sentry since we initially wanted a layer that could very quickly fetch the execution witness

author: kevaundray
content:
There have been a few changes to 8025 (and some changes that would require zkboost as we know it) to change -- do we know what those are?

<@402080271563751435> when we spoke you suggested changing the CL such that its more of a subscription like model, perhaps you and Han can coordinate around this and the other changes needed?

author: kevaundray
content: I think Proof Engine is now very ambiguous 😄

author: taulepton_
content: Yes, happy to collaborate with <@466102668138512394> on this.

author: taulepton_
content:
> I think Proof Engine is now very ambiguous 😄
Certainly agree 😅

author: taulepton_
content: I'll write up some thoughts on the problem space and some solution proposals that we can discuss.

author: han0110
content: That'd be nice, happy to help on anything, mainly would like to implement a reference Proof Engine with Ere, and integrate it into `ethereum-package` once it's defined, so we can test the end-to-end optional proof flow more easily.

author: taulepton_
content: Could you elaborate on what you mean by Proof Engine here. Do you mean zkboost?

author: han0110
content: I mean the entity that provide the RPC endpoint `engine_requestProofsV1`, I think zkboost can be a reference implementation of such service (it's originally built for generic proving API request, but I'm wondering if that's necessary, Ere already provides similar thing)

author: taulepton_
content: Oh ok so you think we will deccomission zkboost and just use Ere directly?

author: han0110
content: Ah zkboost still needs to exist to provide proof of multiple zkVM/guest combination (the `proof_types`), but underlying it forwards to one of Ere server to generating the proof (Ere server is built for single zkVM and initialized with single ELF).

author: taulepton_
content:
So the two main open questions are:
- what do we name zkboost in a general sense (currently we use the term proof engine to refer to both the CL component responsible for handling proofs and zkboost as the proof engine)
- Migrate `engine_requestProofsV1` to a subscription based model. 

Currently the specs expect the proof engine backend / zkboost to send the proof back to the validator for  `engine_requestProofsV1` requests, but this is undeseriable as we have to configure the proof engine backend to know about the validator and it also means its awkward for multiple validators to use a single proof engine backend. Ideally we should migrate to the validator always pulling from the proof engine backend using the `ProofGenId` and some sort of subscription / polling model. 

The subscription model on a high level would work as follows. We call `engine_requestProofsV1` which returns a `ProofGenId`, we then repeatedly poll zkboost (proof engine backend) to give us the latest status of the proof generation process associated with the `ProogGenId`, this polling mechanism should yield the proofs as soon as they become available. I'm not sure about the exact mechanism of how this should work. For example should `engine_requestProofsV1` create a websocket subscription or is http polling a better way to implement this?

author: han0110
content:
> what do we name zkboost in a general sense
yeah I also find this confusing 😅, I tell myself that the CL component is proof service (so it's communicating internally in between beacon node and validator), and zkboost as proof engine spec reference implementation, not sure how to name them better

> Migrate `engine_requestProofsV1` to a subscription based model.
this'd be nice, also struggled configuring the CL endpoint for zkboost when integrating it into `ethereum-package`, I think either SSE listening (on specific `ProofGenId` or all proof updates)  or websocket will be better than polling, since latency is important in this case

author: taulepton_
content:
I think proof service and proof engine could work. I'm also curious about the idea of having the external component being called a proof node. I think this conversation can continue we don't necessarily need to settle now.

<@466102668138512394> SSE is used extensively in other CL components so maybe it makes sense to reuse it here?

author: han0110
content:
proof node sounds more like external component indeed (thought about proving/proof layer but I guess it doesn't make much sense)

> SSE is used extensively in other CL components so maybe it makes sense to reuse it here?
yeah make sense to me, I'd imagine it'd be easy for different programming language to integrate (not sure about WS)

author: taulepton_
content: Yes that makes sense to me. Would you be able to do a quick review of SSE and confirm it is portable across languages and fits our requiements. It'd also be great if you could map out / write an initial spec for how it would work in practice. For example do we expect to have full proofs provided via SSE events or would it just provide events with a reference to the proof and proof gen id?

author: han0110
content: Sure, will do a review on SSE, and think about how the proof engine deliver the proof (will test if it works fine for large data)
